the_signal.headline: Context rot is killing your agent reliability—here's how to fix it
the_signal.body: n8n's deep dive on context engineering reveals that prompt design alone won't save you; token budgeting, context decay over time, and memory management are now first-class architecture problems. If your agents degrade after 50+ tool calls, you're not debugging logic—you're drowning in context bloat. This shifts agentic AI from 'does it work' to 'does it work predictably at scale.'

tool_drop.name: Ternlight
tool_drop.what: 7MB embedding model running in-browser via WASM for real-time vector search without server calls.
tool_drop.why: Eliminates RAG latency and inference cost for GTM workflows that need instant semantic search—perfect for live chat agents, CRM enrichment, or deal scoring that can't wait for API roundtrips.

thread_of_day.title: Small AI models gain traction in places with unreliable networks
thread_of_day.takeaway: Edge deployment and offline-first agent design are no longer niche—they're becoming table stakes for GTM systems in emerging markets and enterprise networks with restricted egress.

quote.text: When a review run falls short, this workflow catches it and proposes a fix to the skill, then writes the improved instructions back once you approve the diff.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Build Self-Improving Agent Skills with cognee

whats_moving.context: n8n's new guide maps in-context buffers vs. vector stores vs. persistent databases—teams are now choosing memory strategy upfront, not bolting it on after production failures.

The architectural guide on error handling for tool calling is shipping with retry strategies and circuit breakers—indicating tool calls are now treated as production infrastructure, not experimental plumbing.
n8n's post on AI security risks and automated response suggests observability + mitigation is moving from compliance checklist to daily ops responsibility for any agent in production.

act_on_this: Audit your agent's memory footprint right now: log token consumption per workflow step, measure context size after 25 and 50 tool calls, and identify where context rot first appears—fix this before scaling.

numbers.stat: 10 vector databases now benchmarked on scale ceiling, metadata filtering, and overhead
numbers.context: Your RAG pipeline's performance isn't tied to model choice anymore—it's tied to which vector DB you picked; misalignment here will kill your GTM agent's latency and cost.
